# Phase 4: XML Comment Editing — Reflow & Edit Bugs

- [Phase 1: Miscellaneous Bugs, Features, and Cleanup](06.misc-bugs-features.md) (Complete)
- [Phase 2: Post-Implementation Bug Fixes](06.misc-bugs-features.02.md) (Complete)

- [Phase 4: XML Comment Editing — Reflow & Edit Bugs](06.misc-bugs-features.04.md) (Complete)

---

## Items In Scope

### Item 1 — Auto-Reflow: Cursor-Leave + Dirty-Flag Model

**Current behavior:** `AutoReflowHandler` fires on every single-char insertion whenever the current line exceeds `maxLineWidth` (1500ms debounce). This causes:
- Mid-typing cursor jumps
- Repeated OmniSharp/test-scanner thrashing — each programmatic `editor.edit()` is indistinguishable from user input
- Aggressive reformatting while the user is mid-thought

**New behavior:**
- **Auto-reflow**: only fires when the cursor **leaves** an XML comment block, and only if at least one edit was made while the cursor was inside that block.
- **Manual reflow** (`reflowComment`, `reflowAllComments`): always reflows, regardless of dirty state.
- Setting `enableReflowWhileTyping` continues to gate auto-reflow behavior. Setting `enableReflowOnPaste` continues to gate paste-based reflow in `smartPaste.ts`.

**Implementation approach:**

`AutoReflowHandler` currently uses a single `onDidChangeTextDocument` listener with a debounce timer. Replace with a two-listener model:

1. **`onDidChangeTextDocument` listener** — only sets dirty flag:
   - If the change is inside a comment block: mark that block dirty.
   - No timer, no immediate reflow.

2. **`onDidChangeTextEditorSelection` listener** — triggers reflow on cursor exit:
   - On each selection change, determine if the cursor **was** in a dirty block (tracked as `lastBlockStart`) and **is now** outside it.
   - If so: run `reflowBlock()` for that block (same logic as current implementation), then clear the dirty flag.
   - Update `lastBlockStart` to the new block (or `undefined` if cursor is outside all comment blocks).

Tracking state per open document (keyed by URI string):
```typescript
interface BlockTracker {
  lastBlockStart: number | undefined;  // start line of last known block cursor was in
  isDirty: boolean;                    // edit was made while in that block
}
private docTrackers = new Map<string, BlockTracker>();
```

Edge cases:
- Cursor stays within same block across multiple edits — only reflow on exit, not per-line-change.
- Document closes while cursor is inside a dirty block — accept the miss (cosmetic only). Optionally flush in `onDidCloseTextDocument`.

**Files:**
- `src/reflow/autoReflow.ts` — replace `onDidChangeTextDocument`-only model with dual-listener + dirty-flag model
- `README.md` — update reflow documentation to reflect new behavior

---

### Item 2 — `<para>` Always Starts on a New Line

**Current behavior:** During reflow, `<para>` opening tags can end up appended to the end of the preceding content line rather than beginning a new line.

**Change:** In `reflowXmlContent()` in `reflowEngine.ts`, when a block tag is encountered and there is already non-empty content in the `result` array, ensure a line break is inserted before pushing the `<para>` opening tag. Specifically, if the last entry in `result` is non-empty when a `<para>` open tag is about to be pushed, insert `result.push('')` first so the tag always starts on its own line.

**Files:**
- `src/reflow/reflowEngine.ts` — add "ensure new line before `<para>` open" guard in the block-tag handler

---

### Item 3 — `<summary>` Open and Close Tags Always on Their Own Lines

**Current behavior:** When `<summary>` content is short enough to fit on a single line, the reflow engine keeps it as `<summary>content</summary>` on one line. The transparent-text opacity effect doesn't render cleanly when the tag and content share a line.

**Change:** In `reflowXmlContent()`, special-case `<summary>` (and optionally `<remarks>`) to always use the multi-line form — `<summary>` on its own line, content line(s) in the middle, `</summary>` on its own line — regardless of content length. Bypass the existing "single line tag with content" path (`result.push('<summary>content</summary>')`) for these tags.

**Files:**
- `src/reflow/reflowEngine.ts` — special-case `<summary>` (and `<remarks>`) to always expand to multi-line form

---

### Item 4 — XML Edit Bugs (Three Observed Issues)

All three issues stem from the reflow engine's block-tag / paragraph state machine not correctly handling `<para>` tags typed mid-block. The parser joins the new tag with surrounding content instead of treating it as a block boundary.

#### Issue 1 — Typing `<para>...</para>` mid-block moves the opening tag

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Start with a multi-line `<summary>` block.
2. Type `<para>` before a word and `</para>` after a sentence end in the middle of the block.
3. Press Enter after `</para>` — the `<para>` opening tag gets moved by reflow to join the preceding content line.

**Root cause:** In the "plain text line" collection loop (`reflowEngine.ts` ~line 210), the loop continues collecting lines even after encountering an inline `<para>` open tag. The accumulated `contentLines` are joined and wrapped before the `<para>` tag is processed, causing the tag to be appended after the merged paragraph.

**Fix:** In the plain-text collection loop, stop collecting when an inline block-level open tag (`<para>`, etc.) is encountered on the current line, so the preceding paragraph flushes before the new tag is handled.

#### Issue 2 — Deleting `///` prefix leaves orphaned `///` on the next line

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Delete the `///` prefix from a line inside a doc comment block.
2. Press Enter — the C# enter rule injects `\n{indent}/// ` on the new line, but the orphaned blank `///` line is not cleaned up.
3. After this point, the auto-`///` prefix insertion stops working for the rest of the session on that file.

**Root cause:** The C# language extension's enter rule inserts `\n{indent}/// ` (a multi-char change including `\n`) when Enter is pressed inside a doc comment. `SmartPasteHandler` sees this change and matches the paste heuristic, firing a full block reflow against an intermediate document state. The reflow leaves the orphaned line. After this, `SmartPasteHandler`'s internal state is corrupted for the session.

**Fix:** In `SmartPasteHandler.handleChange()`, explicitly exclude the enter-rule insertion pattern. A change whose text matches `/^\n\s*\/\/\/ ?$/` (newline + indent + `/// `) is the enter rule firing, not a real paste — skip it.

**Files:**
- `src/reflow/smartPaste.ts` — add enter-rule pattern guard

#### Issue 3 — New `<para>` block merges with preceding `</para>` on same line

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Have a multi-para `<summary>` block.
2. Type `<para>` at the start of a new content line.
3. Backspace the next line's leading word to join it to the current line.
4. Type `</para>` at the end of a sentence on a following line.
5. After reflow, the new `<para>` tag appears immediately after the preceding `</para>` on the same line: `</para><para>...`

**Root cause:** After emitting a `</para>` closing tag, the reflow state machine re-enters the outer loop. The next line begins with `<para>` — but because there is no blank separator in `result` after the closing tag, the engine falls through to the inline-open-tag handler and joins it with the preceding line's context.

**Fix:** After emitting any `</para>` (or other block closing tag), ensure the next entry pushed to `result` is on a fresh line. The fix for Item 2 above (always start `<para>` on new line) likely resolves this as a side effect. Verify after implementing Item 2.

**Files:**
- `src/reflow/reflowEngine.ts` — verify resolved by Item 2 fix; add explicit separator if not

---

## Notes

- Items 2, 3, and 4 are all in `reflowEngine.ts` / `smartPaste.ts` and can be implemented as a single unit.
- Recommend implementing Item 1 first (behavior change) to get a cleaner baseline before fixing Items 2–4 (correctness bugs) — the new cursor-leave model means bugs 2–4 can be tested against one reflow per block-visit rather than one per character.
- Add regression tests for all Issue 1–3 scenarios in `test/`.
- Run `npm run compile` + `npm test` after each item.

When all items are complete and tests pass, update the Phase Work header above to show **Complete** before committing.

---

## Implementation Complete

**Date:** 2026-04-02

### Evidence

`npm run compile` → exit 0 (no TypeScript errors)  
`npm test` → **220/220 tests passed** (11 test files, 0 failures)

### Summary of Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/reflow/autoReflow.ts` | Replaced debounce-on-keypress model with cursor-leave + dirty-flag model (`BlockTracker` interface, `docTrackers` Map, dual-listener: `onDidChangeTextDocument` sets dirty flag, `onDidChangeTextEditorSelection` triggers reflow on exit). Removed cursor save/restore (cursor already away). |
| `src/reflow/reflowEngine.ts` | (Item 2) `<para>` always starts on its own line: blank separator pushed before `<para>` open tag in all three push paths (standalone, inline single-line, inline multi-line). (Item 3) `<summary>` and `<remarks>` always expand to multi-line form regardless of content length. (Issue 1) Inner block-tag content loops now break on inline block-level open tags (e.g. `<para>content`) via `/^<(\w+)[^>]*>/` — previously only broke on standalone tags. (Issue 3) Resolved as side effect of Item 2 blank-separator fix. |
| `src/reflow/smartPaste.ts` | No changes needed — `isEnterRuleInsertion` guard (Issue 2 fix) was already in place. |
| `test/unit/reflowEngine.test.ts` | Updated existing `should handle single-line summary` test to expect new multi-line form. Added 4 regression tests: `<para>` new-line separator after content, `<para>` new-line separator after `</para>`, plain-text loop stops at inline block tag, `<summary>` and `<remarks>` always multi-line. |
